name: node-memfs
title: node-memfs
description:
In-memory filesystem with Node's API
Implements Node's fs API
.
stores files in memory, in buffers and throws same errors as Node.js
has the concepts of i-nodes and implements hard links and soft links
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-memfs because In-memory filesystem with Node's API.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-memfs as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-memfs with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-memfs --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
